rate_limit_per_client_per_min.title: Rate Limit Per Client Per Min
Rate Limit Per Client Per Min

ProfileUpdateRequest.description: Self-service profile edits. Username and role are not editable here.
ProfileUpdateRequest.title: ProfileUpdateRequest

Role.description:
Allowed authenticated roles, highest privilege first.

Privilege ladder (linear): ``owner`` > ``admin`` > ``librarian`` > ``editor`` >
``contributor`` > ``reader``.

- ``reader``: read accessible content only.
- ``contributor``: reader + create/edit/delete **own** papers (``Work.created_by_user_id`` ==
  self); no rack/shelf structural changes.
- ``editor``: contributor + create/edit/delete **any accessible** paper; still no rack/shelf
  structural changes.
- ``librarian``: editor + create/edit/delete racks & shelves and organize papers within them
  (subject to the rack/shelf grant matrix — "not even a librarian without a grant" for
  visible/private targets).
- ``admin``: full administration (users/groups/grants/defaults/agents/AI settings/audit log)
  and bypasses all content ACLs, EXCEPT creating, disabling, deleting or role-changing another
  ``admin`` or the ``owner``.
- ``owner``: single, immutable bootstrap account (``make bootstrap-admin``). It can never be
  disabled, deleted, role-changed, or disable itself, and is the only account that may manage
  ``admin`` accounts. There is exactly one owner. Bypasses all content ACLs.
Role.title: Role

SavedFilterParams.description:
The structured (non-free-text) part of a saved Library query.

Mirrors the explicit query params ``list_works``/``build_works_query`` accept. ``missing`` is a
list of Work field names to filter on absence of (``title``/``abstract``/``year``/…).
SavedFilterParams.title: SavedFilterParams

WorkRackRef.description: A rack containing one of a paper's (see-able) shelves, SEE-filtered (D32 library column).
WorkRackRef.title: WorkRackRef

WorkShelfMembership.description:
A shelf that contains a paper, with the caller's modify-flag and its containing racks.

``can_modify`` reflects ``access.can_modify_shelf`` for the caller (the librarian-floor
STRUCTURE rule), so the UI can gate the per-shelf Remove button. ``racks`` is filtered to the
racks the caller may SEE (a shelf may sit in 0..N racks).
WorkShelfMembership.title: WorkShelfMembership

WorkShelfRackRef.description: A rack that contains one of a paper's shelves (SEE-filtered).
WorkShelfRackRef.title: WorkShelfRackRef

WorkShelfRef.description: A shelf a paper sits on, SEE-filtered (id + name only; D32 library column).
WorkShelfRef.title: WorkShelfRef

The owner's password is never resettable here; resetting an admin's is owner-only.

post.description: Request enrollment with an owner-issued token; creates a pending agent (202).

Report this agent's files + their processing/teleport state (for the agent's own status view).

Includes the linked Work's title + authors (#11) so the agent GUI can search/sort by them.

schema.title: Response List Agent File Status Api V1 Agents Files Get

post.description:
`index_and_extract`: agent uploads a PDF; the server extracts it, keeps a preview, and
discards the PDF afterwards (only the reference + metadata remain).

schema.title: Response Upload For Extraction Api V1 Agents Files  Local File Id  Extract Post

post.description:
Agent-*initiated* teleport (#12): the agent pushes a file directly (no prior user request).

Allowed only when the owner has granted ``can_teleport``. The server verifies the hash, stores
the managed file + Work, and enqueues extraction — mirroring a user-requested teleport.

schema.title: Response Offer Teleport Api V1 Agents Files  Local File Id  Offer Teleport Post

schema.title: Response Receive Manifest Api V1 Agents Manifest Post

Return the calling agent's identity, approval, and granted privileges (reachability check).

Also reports ``max_batch_items`` — the server's import-batch cap (D1) — so the agent can split
an oversized scan into ≤cap manifests before pushing them.

schema.title: Response Agent Self Api V1 Agents Me Get

post.description: Deprecated registration stub; use ``/enroll-request`` + owner approval instead.

schema.title: Response Unblock Teleport Api V1 Agents Teleports  Local File Id  Unblock Post

post.description:
Generate (replacing prior) an extractive summary over a library/shelf/rack scope.

Access control: a shelf/rack scope requires SEE on that container, and only papers the caller
may SEE feed the summary.

Run the topic model over a scope (TF-IDF baseline or embedding backend) + store assignments.

Access control: a shelf/rack scope requires SEE on that container, and only papers the caller
may SEE are clustered.

Create a tag from a topic and apply it to the topic's works (SPEC §8.15.3).

Only papers the caller may SEE are tagged.

schema.title: Response Accept Topic As Tag Api V1 Ai Topics Accept As Tag Post

post.description:
Create a shelf from a topic and add the topic's works to it (SPEC §8.15.3).

Creating a shelf is a librarian+ action; only papers the caller may SEE are added. The new
shelf takes the global default access level.

schema.title: Response Create Shelf From Topic Api V1 Ai Topics Create Shelf Post

post.description: Change the caller's password and revoke their other sessions (the current one is kept).

schema.title: Response Change Own Password Api V1 Auth Change Password Post

post.description:
Authenticate a user and create a revocable bearer-token session.

Repeated failures for a username are throttled (SPEC §7.2): after the configured number of
failures within the window the account is temporarily locked with a ``Retry-After`` hint.
Invalidate the current session/token.

schema.title: Response Logout Api V1 Auth Logout Post

get.description: Return the authenticated caller's identity and profile (SPEC §9.3).

schema.title: Response Whoami Api V1 Auth Me Get

patch.description: Update the caller's own editable profile (display name, email, papers per page).

schema.title: Response Update Me Api V1 Auth Me Patch

get.description: List duplicate/version candidates for review (hiding candidates that touch a hidden work).

schema.title: Response List Duplicate Candidates Api V1 Duplicates Get

post.description:
Scan selected or all known work/file identities for duplicate candidates.

Only papers/files the caller may SEE are scanned (a full-library scan skips hidden works).

patch.description: Update review status or apply a duplicate/version review action.

post.description:
Export citations for a work, shelf, rack, search result, selection, or saved filter.

Access control: the export is filtered to papers the caller may SEE; a shelf/rack scope
requires SEE on that container. A ``saved_filter`` scope (Phase B7) loads the caller's own
filter (404 on a missing/foreign one), resolves it to work ids clamped to the caller's visible
set, and exports those.

get.description:
List the citation styles available for the ``styled`` export format.

Returned as ``[{"value", "label"}, ...]`` so the frontend can populate the style selector
dynamically instead of hard-coding the list.

schema.title: Response List Citation Styles Api V1 Exports Styles Get

get.description: List file metadata for the library file view (filtered to files the caller may see).

schema.title: Response List Files Api V1 Files Get

get.description: Return file metadata and quick preview text.

post.description:
Queue GROBID extraction for a file (runs in the background worker).

``force_ocr=true`` re-runs OCRmyPDF even when the text layer looks fine / OCR is disabled —
the manual "Force OCR" action for a scanned PDF that came out textless (#22).

schema.title: Response Extract File Api V1 Files  File Id  Extract Post

get.description: Stream a PDF from a server-folder or managed-library location (records a view audit event).

Return the served PDF's plain text (SEE-gated like ``/stream``).

Uses PyMuPDF's native text layer; when that is sparse (a scanned PDF), it falls back to
on-the-fly OCR (``get_textpage_ocr``) in the admin-configured OCR language. Powers the reader's
search / "copy text" fallback for OCR'd or scanned PDFs. Never raises on OCR failure — it
degrades to whatever native text (possibly empty) was found.

post.description:
Build a scoped citation graph (nodes = works, edges = bibliography citations).

Access control: hidden works never appear as nodes/edges, and a shelf/rack scope requires SEE
on that container.

Build a scoped embedding-similarity graph (nodes = papers, edges = semantic similarity, #6).

Access control mirrors the citation graph: hidden papers never appear, and a shelf/rack scope
requires SEE on that container. Edge weight is cosine similarity (inverted semantic distance);
edges are kNN-sparsified.

get.description: Return basic service health.

post.description:
Commit confirmed batch drafts into works (deduped) and optionally add them to a shelf.

Gated at the contributor floor; import-to-shelf additionally requires shelf modify access
(enforced inside the shared ACL helper — a 404/403 there rolls the whole commit back).

Preview a batch of raw citation lines (NO writes). Returns 200 even on a partial/degraded run.

``engine="lookup"`` searches Crossref/OpenAlex/Semantic Scholar per line (timeboxed by the
web_find wall-clock budget); ``engine="grobid"`` parses every line in one processCitationList
call and degrades to title-only (with ``grobid_unavailable``) when GROBID is down.

get.description:
List import batches (newest first) for the graph's import-batch scope picker.

Access-filtered (Phase B6/H): owner/admin see all batches; everyone else sees only batches they
created. Each row carries the current work count for a human-readable picker label.

schema.title: Response List Import Batches Api V1 Imports Batches Get

post.description: Create works from pasted/uploaded BibTeX content.
Create works from pasted/uploaded CSL-JSON content.
Import PDFs from a configured server-folder source.

Create or locate a work from an arXiv id or DOI, then enrich from external sources.

Returns the work id and a flag indicating whether a new work was created.  If a work
with the same identifier already exists it is returned unchanged (idempotent).
Create works from pasted/uploaded RIS content.

Request a teleport of an agent-indexed file to the managed library (user-authorised).

Marks the manifest entry as requested; the agent then pushes the bytes to
``/agents/teleports/{local_file_id}/content``.

schema.title: Response Request Teleport Api V1 Imports Teleport Post

post.description:
Upload a single PDF to the managed library (content-addressed, auto-deduped).

``target_shelf_id`` (Phase J item 6) optionally adds the uploaded paper to a shelf. The work is
minted by ``import_uploaded_pdf`` at request time (a new FileWorkLink, or the pre-existing one
for a deduped upload), so we resolve it and add it through the shared ACL-checked helper *here*
— synchronous-at-upload rather than the design's worker-deferred option, since the work id is
already available and we keep the actor's request/ACL context (see report deviation note). A
missing shelf / lack of modify access (404/403) aborts the upload via the helper.

get.description: Return import batch status and stats (owner/admin see all; others only their own).
Return queue counts, worker count, and recent jobs (or availability=False).

schema.title: Response Get Job Status Api V1 Jobs Get

post.description: Clear finished/failed (and optionally queued) job history. Running jobs are untouched.

schema.title: Response Clear Job History Api V1 Jobs Clear Post

post.description:
Empty the pending job queue (admin). Running jobs are untouched; returns how many dropped.

Degrades gracefully (never 500) when Redis is unreachable — reports ``available: false``.

schema.title: Response Clear Queue Api V1 Jobs Clear Queue Post

post.description:
Re-enqueue extraction for every file still owed one (the D7 recovery sweep, on demand).

Idempotent: a file already queued/running is left alone. Degrades gracefully (never 500) when
the queue is offline — reports ``redis_reachable: false`` instead.

schema.title: Response Reprocess Pending Api V1 Jobs Reprocess Pending Post

post.description:
Recover stuck jobs (admin): requeue jobs stranded as started and clear failed history.

Cannot restart the worker *processes* (they run under the supervisor in the worker container);
the response ``note`` says a full reset is ``docker compose restart worker``. Degrades
gracefully (never 500) when Redis is unreachable.

schema.title: Response Reset Workers Api V1 Jobs Reset Workers Post

get.description: Return the caller's stored preferences (empty defaults if none saved yet).

put.description: Replace the caller's preferences blob and return what was stored.

get.description: List racks the caller may see.

schema.title: Response List Racks Api V1 Racks Get

post.description: Create a rack (librarian+). Defaults to the global default access level.

delete.description:
Hard-delete a rack (requires modify access).

``delete_shelves=false`` (default): the rack and its rack↔shelf links are removed; the shelves
themselves survive (they simply leave this rack — a shelf with no rack is fine).
``delete_shelves=true``: each associated shelf the caller may modify is ALSO hard-deleted (its
papers left with no shelf fall back to the default shelf), except the default shelf, which is
never deleted. Distinct from archiving (PATCH status), which keeps the rack.

patch.description: Edit or archive a rack (requires modify access to this rack).

get.description: List shelves in a rack (filtered to shelves the caller may see).

schema.title: Response List Rack Shelves Api V1 Racks  Rack Id  Shelves Get

post.description: Add a shelf to a rack (requires modify access to both rack and shelf).

delete.description: Remove a shelf from a rack (requires modify access to the rack).

get.description: List the caller's saved filters (ordered by name).

schema.title: Response List Filters Api V1 Saved Filters Get

post.description: Create a saved filter owned by the caller (409 on a duplicate name).

delete.description: Delete the caller's saved filter (404 if it isn't theirs).

put.description: Update the caller's saved filter (404 if it isn't theirs; 409 on a duplicate name).

post.description:
Unified search: lexical (BM25F+), semantic (dense), or hybrid (RRF fusion, default).

Read-only. Access control is applied inside each engine (results are filtered to papers the
caller may SEE). Semantic/hybrid modes report which embedding provider actually served the
ranking vs the one configured, so a silent hash-BOW fallback is visible.

Embed any works missing a vector for the active provider (owner/editor). Returns count added.

Embeddings are normally created on import in the background; this rebuilds them on demand (e.g.
after a bulk import while the worker was down, or after switching embedding providers). Also
backfills chunk-level embeddings for the active model (no-op unless a real model with a pgvector
column is active on Postgres).

Surfaces provider provenance so the UI can warn when the requested provider (e.g. an Ollama
model) was unavailable and the reindex silently ran under the hash-BOW fallback instead.

schema.title: Response Reindex Embeddings Api V1 Search Reindex Post

post.description:
Rank works by similarity to a free-text query. Read-only: embeddings are built off this
path (on import / via /search/reindex), so a search never writes to the database.

Access control: results are filtered to papers the caller may SEE, applied *inside* the ranking
(selectivity-adaptive: exact over a small visible set, else ANN with an allow-list) so the
visible top-N is exact — no over-fetch heuristic.

Warm the BM25F+ lexical index into memory (call when the library view opens) so the first
lexical/hybrid search is hot. Idempotent — the index is rebuilt only when the corpus changed.

schema.title: Response Warm Search Api V1 Search Warm Post

get.description: List shelves the caller may see, each annotated with the caller's ``can_modify`` flag.

schema.title: Response List Shelves Api V1 Shelves Get

post.description: Create a shelf (librarian+). Defaults to the global default access level.

delete.description:
Hard-delete a shelf (requires modify access). This removes the shelf and its memberships.

No free-floating papers (#1): a paper that was ONLY on this shelf is moved to the default
shelf; a paper also on other shelves simply loses this association. The default shelf itself
cannot be deleted (it is the fallback home). Distinct from archiving (PATCH status), which keeps
the shelf.

patch.description: Edit or archive a shelf (requires modify access to this shelf).

get.description: List works in a shelf (requires SEE on the shelf; works the caller can't see are hidden).

schema.title: Response List Shelf Works Api V1 Shelves  Shelf Id  Works Get

post.description: Add a work to a shelf (requires modify access to the shelf).

delete.description: Remove a work from a shelf (requires modify access to the shelf).

get.description: List configured sources.

schema.title: Response List Sources Api V1 Sources Get

post.description: Create a source for a preconfigured server-folder alias.

get.description: List tags.

schema.title: Response List Tags Api V1 Tags Get

post.description: Create or return a tag by normalized name.

delete.description: Remove a tag from an entity (requires modify access to that entity).

post.description: Attach a tag to a work, shelf, or rack (requires modify access to that entity).

get.description:
List/search works by basic metadata and extraction/metadata completeness (paginated).

``q`` supports structured operators (``author:`` ``year:>=2020`` ``venue:`` ``tag:`` ``type:``
``title:`` ``doi:`` ``arxiv:`` ``status:`` ``shelf:`` ``rack:`` ``cites:`` ``cited_by_local:``
``has:pdf|references|notes|annotations|summary|abstract``); the leftover free text matches
title/abstract/DOI/arXiv/venue. Explicit query params (``has_pdf`` etc.) still work and take
precedence. ``shelf:``/``rack:`` and ``cites:``/``cited_by_local:`` compose ON TOP of the SEE
filter (only see-able shelves/racks contribute; they never widen visibility).

Pagination (D18): ``per_page`` overrides the caller's ``papers_per_page`` preference (else the
server default); the effective size is clamped to the admin global maximum. ``page`` is clamped
into ``1..pages``. The response envelope carries ``items``/``total``/``page``/``pages``/
``per_page``. Each item also carries its SEE-filtered ``shelves``/``racks`` (D32).

Access control: only papers the caller may SEE are returned (most-permissive governing shelf;
loose papers are open; admin/owner see all). The filter body is ``build_works_query`` (reused by
the saved-filter/graph/export resolvers); ``list_works`` adds sort + pagination.

post.description: Create a work manually (owned by the actor so contributors may edit their own).

get.description:
Search annotations across all works by selected text / note body (SPEC §8.8.7).

Restricted to annotations on papers the caller may SEE.

schema.title: Response Search Annotations Api V1 Works Annotations Search Get

post.description:
Create a library work from an unresolved citation reference (SPEC §8.9 import-missing-ref).

Idempotent: if the reference already resolves to a work, that work is returned. The new work is
owned by the actor (``created_by_user_id``) so a contributor may later edit their own import.

get.description:
Return the manual reading queue (status='reading'), ordered by queue_position then recency.

Filtered to papers the caller may SEE.

schema.title: Response Reading Queue Api V1 Works Reading Queue Get

post.description:
Set the reading-queue order to the given work id sequence (SPEC §8.17.1).

Each listed paper must be modifiable by the caller (own-only for contributors).

schema.title: Response Reorder Reading Queue Api V1 Works Reading Queue Reorder Post

delete.description:
Delete a paper and its dependent rows (contributors may delete only their own papers).

Removes links and derived data (memberships, tags, assertions, summaries, embeddings,
references, mentions, annotations, versions, duplicate candidates). The underlying File
rows and managed PDFs are content-addressed and may be shared, so they are kept; only the
file↔work links are removed.

get.description:
Return one work, recording a debounced `paper.viewed` audit event (§7.6).

The event (an INSERT + COMMIT) is skipped when the same user viewed the same work within
``_VIEW_DEBOUNCE_S`` (E2), so normal browsing doesn't write a row per click.

patch.description: Edit a work manually (contributors may edit only their own papers).

get.description: List annotations stored separately from a work's PDFs.

schema.title: Response List Work Annotations Api V1 Works  Work Id  Annotations Get

post.description: Create a reader annotation without modifying the source PDF.

get.description: Export a work's annotations as Markdown or plain text (SPEC §8.17.4).

schema.title: Response Export Work Annotations Api V1 Works  Work Id  Annotations Export Get

delete.description: Delete a reader annotation (404 when it belongs to a different paper).

get.description: Return in-text citation contexts for one work.

schema.title: Response Get Work Citation Contexts Api V1 Works  Work Id  Citation Contexts Get

post.description: Queue external metadata enrichment for a work (needs a DOI or arXiv id).

schema.title: Response Enrich Work Endpoint Api V1 Works  Work Id  Enrich Post

post.description:
Queue GROBID extraction for every file attached to a work.

404 if the paper is missing; ``{status: "no_files"}`` when nothing is attached; 503 if the
extraction queue is unavailable. Per-file extraction is still available via /files/{id}/extract.

schema.title: Response Extract Work Endpoint Api V1 Works  Work Id  Extract Post

get.description: List the files attached to a work (via FileWorkLink).

schema.title: Response List Work Files Api V1 Works  Work Id  Files Get

post.description: Upload a PDF and attach it to an existing work (so a manual work isn't a dead end).

delete.description:
Detach a file from a paper (#17). The File itself is retained (it may back other papers);
only the link is removed. If it was the paper's main file, the pointer is cleared.

post.description:
Search legitimate scholarly sources for candidate matches for a paper (#5).

Read-only egress: never stores anything and never fails the whole search when a source is
down — a degraded source is reported in ``degraded_sources``. Returns a (possibly empty)
ranked candidate list.

Download 0…N selected candidate PDFs and attach them to a paper (find-on-web v2).

Per-item status: ``attached`` / ``deduped`` / ``manual_upload_needed`` / ``error`` /
``blocked`` / ``needs_confirmation``. The host classification + download-policy mode gate run
on every redirect hop inside ``download_and_attach``: a shadow-library / private-IP / bad-scheme
host is ``blocked`` (stores nothing) in every mode; in ``unrestricted`` mode an unknown public
host returns ``needs_confirmation`` (with the URL) unless the item set ``confirmed=true``. A
failed item never aborts the others; an empty ``items`` list is a no-op.

Stream find-on-web search progress as NDJSON (find-on-web v2). Contributor-gated.

Emits one JSON object per line (``application/x-ndjson``):
  * ``{"type":"source","source":<name>,"status":"querying"}`` as each source starts,
  * ``{"type":"source","source":<name>,"status":"done","count":N}`` / ``"status":"failed"``
    as each source finishes,
  * a final ``{"type":"result","candidates":[...],"degraded_sources":[...],
    "queried_sources":[...]}`` line.
Queue per-paper keyword extraction for a work (re-runs RAKE over its text; no precondition).

schema.title: Response Keywords Work Endpoint Api V1 Works  Work Id  Keywords Post

put.description: Choose a paper's primary file for one-click 'Read' (#16). The file must be attached.

get.description: Return metadata assertions for a work, grouped by field, flagging conflicts.

schema.title: Response Get Work Metadata Api V1 Works  Work Id  Metadata Get

post.description: Lock or unlock a single field so enrichment won't overwrite it (SPEC §8.12).
Choose an assertion as the canonical value for its field (a review action).

delete.description:
Delete one metadata assertion so a user can resolve a conflict by removing wrong entries.

404 if the assertion is missing or belongs to a different paper. Gated like the other metadata
mutations (contributor floor + own-only ``_guard_modify_work``).

Canonical re-resolution: if the deleted assertion was the field's canonical one, the most
recently retrieved of the *remaining* assertions for that field is promoted to canonical (and
applied to the Work for promotable fields), mirroring ``select_metadata_assertion``. If no
assertions remain for the field, nothing is left canonical and the Work's column value is kept
as-is (we never blank out an existing title/abstract just because its provenance rows are gone).

get.description: Return the parsed bibliography (extracted references) for a work.

schema.title: Response List Work References Api V1 Works  Work Id  References Get

get.description:
Return papers most similar to this one, with a "why related" reason (SPEC §8.17.2).

The reason is the keywords the two papers share (there are no author entities to compare),
or the embedding-similarity score when they share none. Both the source paper and every
related paper are filtered to those the caller may SEE.

schema.title: Response Related Papers Api V1 Works  Work Id  Related Get

get.description:
Return every shelf containing this paper that the caller may SEE ("Where is this?").

Guarded like ``get_work`` (404 if the paper is missing or the caller can't see it). Each shelf
is annotated with its ``access_level``, a ``can_modify`` flag (the librarian-floor STRUCTURE
rule via ``access.can_modify_shelf`` — NOT the paper-edit rule), and its containing racks,
themselves SEE-filtered (a private rack without a grant is omitted even when the shelf shows).

schema.title: Response List Work Shelves Api V1 Works  Work Id  Shelves Get

get.description: List stored summaries for a work (newest first).

schema.title: Response List Summaries Api V1 Works  Work Id  Summaries Get

post.description: Generate a local (no-LLM) summary for a work and store it with provenance.
Queue per-paper topic modeling for a work (representative topic terms; no precondition).

schema.title: Response Topic Work Endpoint Api V1 Works  Work Id  Topics Post
